opportunity.title: Jeriko – an AI agent that runs directly inside your OS
opportunity.why: User looking for AI agent/tool — kbot could be relevant
opportunity.action: REVIEW: Consider replying with kbot if genuinely helpful

actions.draft: The architecture demands strict sandboxing—if the agent can invoke arbitrary APIs, you need a kernel-enforced permission layer, not just user-space ACLs. I've seen agents fail because they don't handle resource contention; a real-time response path that doesn't deadlock under load is non-trivial.
actions.reasoning: I have hands-on experience building multi-provider agent routing, tool orchestration, and OS-integrated agent systems, which directly relates to the architectural discussion here.
